seoTitle: Deakin vs Dickson — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Deakin and Dickson. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Deakin and Dickson.
heading: Deakin vs Dickson — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Deakin, Australian Capital Territory and Dickson, Australian Capital Territory. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Deakin has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Dickson (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Deakin or Dickson safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Deakin has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Dickson scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Deakin scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Dickson scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Deakin or Dickson?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Deakin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
